name: Lionel Messi
story: He left Rosario for Barcelona at 13 to receive growth-hormone treatment, growing up bilingual in Spanish and Catalan far from home.

name: Lautaro Martínez
story: After moving to Inter Milan he learned Italian on the job, conducting interviews in his adopted language within a couple of seasons.

name: Julián Álvarez
story: He moved from small-town Calchín to Manchester and then Madrid, picking up English along the way as a young striker abroad.

name: Enzo Fernández
story: A move from River Plate to the Premier League pushed him to learn English quickly to settle into life in London.

name: Kylian Mbappé
story: Son of a Cameroonian father and Algerian mother, he grew up in the Paris suburb of Bondy and added Spanish and English to his French.

name: Antoine Griezmann
story: He left France at 14 for Real Sociedad's academy in the Basque Country, becoming so fluent he is often mistaken for a native Spanish speaker.

name: Ousmane Dembélé
story: His years at Barcelona immersed him in Spanish after a childhood in Évreux with Mauritanian, Senegalese and Malian roots.

name: Aurélien Tchouaméni
story: Born in Rouen to Cameroonian parents, he took up Spanish after signing for Real Madrid.

name: N'Golo Kanté
story: The son of Malian immigrants who worked as a refuse collector as a teenager, he learned English during his title-winning years in the Premier League.

name: Vinícius Júnior
story: He moved from Rio to Madrid at 18 and learned Spanish immersed in the Real Madrid dressing room.

name: Rodrygo
story: Arriving at Real Madrid as a teenager, he picked up Spanish alongside compatriot Vinícius in their new city.

name: Neymar
story: Spells at Barcelona and Paris Saint-Germain gave him Spanish and a working command of French on top of his native Portuguese.

name: Casemiro
story: From São José dos Campos he moved to Madrid and then Manchester, adding Spanish and English through years in Europe.

name: Erling Haaland
story: Born in Leeds while his father played in England, he grew up bilingual and speaks fluent English with a hint of Yorkshire.

name: Harry Kane
story: After moving to Bayern Munich he committed to learning German, taking lessons to integrate into Bavarian life.

name: Jude Bellingham
story: He left Birmingham as a teenager for Dortmund, learning German, then Spanish after joining Real Madrid.

name: Bukayo Saka
story: Born in Ealing to Nigerian parents, he grew up connected to his Yoruba heritage in London.

name: Phil Foden
story: A lifelong Stockport local, he rose through Manchester City's academy without ever needing to leave his home region.

name: Cristiano Ronaldo
story: He left the island of Madeira for Lisbon at 12 and then Manchester at 18, learning English and later Spanish across his career.

name: Bruno Fernandes
story: He moved to Italy as a teenager, learning Italian in Serie A before adding English at Manchester United.

name: Bernardo Silva
story: From Lisbon to Monaco to Manchester, he became comfortably multilingual across French, English and Spanish.

name: Rafael Leão
story: A move to AC Milan saw him learn Italian and embrace life in the city, even releasing music in multiple languages.

name: Kevin De Bruyne
story: Raised in multilingual Belgium and schooled partly in English, he switches effortlessly between four languages.

name: Romelu Lukaku
story: The son of a Congolese footballer, he famously speaks six languages and learned each one to feel at home in a new club.

name: Pedri
story: He moved from the Canary Islands to mainland Spain as a teenager to chase his Barcelona dream.

name: Gavi
story: He left his Andalusian hometown of Los Palacios at 11 to live in Barcelona's youth residence, La Masia.

name: Rodri
story: He completed a business degree while learning English after moving to Manchester City.

name: Lamine Yamal
story: He grew up in the diverse Barcelona suburb of Rocafonda with Moroccan and Equatorial Guinean roots, bilingual in Spanish and Catalan.

name: Álvaro Morata
story: Stints in England and Italy left him fluent in English and Italian, and he married an Italian.

name: Virgil van Dijk
story: Of Surinamese-Dutch heritage, he honed his English during his rise through Scottish and English football.

name: Frenkie de Jong
story: He learned Spanish after joining Barcelona, conducting press conferences in his new language.

name: Memphis Depay
story: With Ghanaian heritage and clubs in England, France and Spain, he learned Spanish at Barcelona while writing music in English.

name: Cody Gakpo
story: Of Togolese and Dutch descent, he developed fluent English before moving to Liverpool.

name: Luka Modrić
story: He spent part of his childhood as a war refugee living in a hotel, later learning Spanish and English across his career.

name: Joško Gvardiol
story: A move to RB Leipzig saw him pick up German before adding English in Manchester.

name: Christian Pulisic
story: Of Croatian descent, he moved alone to Germany at 16 and learned German, then Italian after joining AC Milan.

name: Weston McKennie
story: He spent part of his childhood on a US military base in Germany, later learning German at Schalke and Italian at Juventus.

name: Tyler Adams
story: He moved from New York to Leipzig as a young man and learned German in the Bundesliga.

name: Giovanni Reyna
story: Son of former US captain Claudio Reyna, he was born in England, raised partly in Germany, and learned German at Dortmund.

name: Yunus Musah
story: Born in New York to Ghanaian parents, raised in Italy and London and developed in Valencia, he speaks four languages fluently.

name: Folarin Balogun
story: Born in New York to Nigerian parents and raised in London, he learned French after moving to Monaco.

name: Timothy Weah
story: Son of Liberian legend and president George Weah, he learned French coming through Paris Saint-Germain's academy.

name: Sergiño Dest
story: Born in the Netherlands to a Surinamese-American father, he grew up bilingual before choosing to represent the USA.

name: Hirving Lozano
story: He learned Dutch at PSV Eindhoven and Italian at Napoli after leaving Mexico City.

name: Edson Álvarez
story: A move to Ajax taught him Dutch before he switched to English in the Premier League.

name: Santiago Giménez
story: He picked up Dutch quickly after joining Feyenoord and becoming a fan favourite in Rotterdam.

name: Alphonso Davies
story: Born in a Ghanaian refugee camp to Liberian parents, he moved to Canada at five and learned German at Bayern Munich.

name: Jonathan David
story: Born in Brooklyn, raised in Haiti and then Ottawa, he sharpened his French playing in Belgium and France.

name: Takefusa Kubo
story: He trained at Barcelona's academy from age nine, becoming fluent in Spanish before returning to play in Spain.

name: Wataru Endō
story: He learned German captaining Stuttgart before moving to Liverpool and adding English.

name: Kaoru Mitoma
story: He wrote a university thesis on dribbling before moving to England and learning English at Brighton.

name: Son Heung-min
story: He moved alone to Hamburg at 16 to chase a football career, learning German and later fluent English in London.

name: Kim Min-jae
story: He learned Italian in Naples and German in Munich after leaving Asia for European football.

name: Achraf Hakimi
story: Born in Madrid to Moroccan immigrant parents who worked as a cleaner and street vendor, he grew up between Spanish and Arabic.

name: Hakim Ziyech
story: Born in the Netherlands to a Moroccan family, he grew up bilingual and chose to represent his parents' homeland.

name: Sofyan Amrabat
story: Raised in the Netherlands by Moroccan parents, he later learned Italian playing for Fiorentina.

name: Yassine Bounou
story: Born in Montreal and raised in Morocco, he learned Spanish during his long career in Spain.

name: Sadio Mané
story: He left his rural village against his family's wishes to chase football in Dakar, later learning English at Liverpool.

name: Kalidou Koulibaly
story: Born in France to Senegalese parents, he learned Italian over years in Naples before choosing Senegal internationally.

name: Mohamed Salah
story: He commuted hours daily from his Nile Delta village to train as a boy, later mastering English as a Liverpool icon.

name: Federico Valverde
story: He moved from Montevideo to Madrid as a teenager and added English to his Spanish at Real Madrid.

name: Darwin Núñez
story: From a working-class family in Artigas, he moved through Spain and Portugal before learning English at Liverpool.

name: Luis Díaz
story: He grew up in the Indigenous Wayuu community of La Guajira, learning English after his move to Liverpool.

name: James Rodríguez
story: He moved abroad as a teenager to Argentina and then Portugal, picking up Portuguese and English across continents.

name: Thomas Müller
story: A lifelong Bayern Munich player, he picked up English to communicate with a constantly international dressing room.

name: Jamal Musiala
story: Born in Germany and raised in England with Nigerian heritage, he grew up fully bilingual and chose Germany over England.

name: Kai Havertz
story: He learned English after moving to London to play for Chelsea and then Arsenal.

name: İlkay Gündoğan
story: Born in Germany to a Turkish family, he grew up bilingual and later learned Spanish during a season at Barcelona.

name: Dušan Vlahović
story: He moved from Belgrade to Italy as a teenager and learned Italian rising through Fiorentina and Juventus.

name: Sergej Milinković-Savić
story: Born in Spain while his father played there, he later learned Italian over years in Rome with Lazio.

name: Megan Rapinoe
story: A spell at Lyon in France broadened her horizons even as English remained her main language.

name: Alex Morgan
story: She picked up some French during a season at Lyon and has written children's books to encourage young readers.

name: Sophia Smith
story: A Colorado native, she rose through the US system and Stanford before becoming a national-team star.

name: Lindsey Horan
story: She skipped college to move to Paris Saint-Germain at 18, becoming fluent in French while living alone in France.

name: Trinity Rodman
story: Daughter of NBA legend Dennis Rodman, she became the youngest player drafted in NWSL history.

name: Naomi Girma
story: The daughter of Ethiopian immigrants in San Jose, she grew up speaking Amharic at home alongside English.

name: Crystal Dunn
story: A versatile New Yorker, she spent a season in England with Chelsea before returning to the US league.

name: Rose Lavelle
story: The Cincinnati playmaker had a stint at Manchester City, experiencing English football firsthand.

name: Aitana Bonmatí
story: Raised near Barcelona, she is bilingual in Catalan and Spanish and gave her Ballon d'Or speech partly in English.

name: Alexia Putellas
story: A Catalan from Mollet del Vallès, she grew up bilingual and added English as the global face of Barcelona Femení.

name: Salma Paralluelo
story: A former teenage track athlete from Zaragoza, she chose football and moved to Barcelona to develop.

name: Jenni Hermoso
story: The Madrid-born striker played in Sweden and Mexico, broadening her experience beyond Spanish football.

name: Olga Carmona
story: The Seville-born defender scored the goal that won Spain the 2023 World Cup final.

name: Sam Kerr
story: Of Anglo-Indian heritage, she switched from Australian Rules football to soccer and built her career in the US and England.

name: Mary Fowler
story: Of Papua New Guinean and Irish heritage, she spent part of her childhood in Ireland before settling in Australia.

name: Hayley Raso
story: She moved to Real Madrid after recovering from a serious back injury, picking up Spanish in the capital.

name: Caitlin Foord
story: From Sydney's south coast, she has played in Japan, the US and England across a globe-trotting career.

name: Ada Hegerberg
story: She moved to France as a teenager and became fluent in French over a decade dominating with Lyon.

name: Guro Reiten
story: She studied teaching back home before moving to London to star for Chelsea, sharpening her English.

name: Lena Oberdorf
story: One of Germany's youngest stars, she added English as her international profile grew.

name: Alexandra Popp
story: A trained animal keeper, the Germany captain learned English as a long-serving international leader.

name: Sara Däbritz
story: She moved to Paris Saint-Germain and then Lyon, learning French during her years in France.

name: Wendie Renard
story: She left her home island of Martinique at 16, arriving in mainland France alone to pursue football with Lyon.

name: Eugénie Le Sommer
story: France's record scorer from Brittany, she briefly played in the US before returning to Lyon.

name: Kadidiatou Diani
story: Born in the Paris suburbs with Malian heritage, she grew up in the city's vibrant banlieue football culture.

name: Grace Geyoro
story: Born in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, she moved to France as a young child and rose through PSG.

name: Lauren James
story: Sister of England men's defender Reece James, she emerged as one of the Lionesses' brightest talents.

name: Lucy Bronze
story: Of Portuguese heritage, she learned Spanish at Barcelona and has spoken about her late-diagnosed autism.

name: Keira Walsh
story: She moved from Manchester to Barcelona for a world-record fee and began learning Spanish in Catalonia.

name: Millie Bright
story: She captained England at the 2023 World Cup after overcoming serious childhood asthma in Yorkshire.

name: Mary Earps
story: She rebuilt her career in Germany with Wolfsburg, learning German before returning to England as a World Cup star.

name: Lauren Hemp
story: From the Norfolk countryside, she moved to Manchester City as a teenager to chase the professional game.

name: Alessia Russo
story: Of Italian descent, she played college soccer in the United States before returning to star for England.

name: Hinata Miyazawa
story: She won the Golden Boot at the 2023 World Cup, lighting up the tournament with five goals.

name: Saki Kumagai
story: A veteran of Lyon and Bayern Munich, she learned both French and German during a long European career.

name: Marta
story: She left a poor town in Alagoas as a teenager, moved to Sweden and learned Swedish and English as a global icon.

name: Asisat Oshoala
story: Africa's most decorated women's footballer, she left Lagos for England, China and then Barcelona in Spain.

name: Linda Caicedo
story: A cancer survivor at 15, she became a teenage sensation and moved to Real Madrid soon after the World Cup.

name: Khadija Shaw
story: From Spanish Town, she lost several family members to violence before college soccer in the US and stardom in England.

name: Stina Blackstenius
story: She moved to London to play for Arsenal, where English became part of her daily life.

name: Kosovare Asllani
story: Daughter of Kosovo-Albanian refugees, she grew up multilingual and added Spanish and Italian playing for Madrid and Milan.

name: Fridolina Rolfö
story: She learned German in the Bundesliga and Spanish at Barcelona during a career spanning three countries.

name: Debinha
story: She played in China and starred for years in the US league, learning English far from her native Brazil.

name: Mauricio Pochettino
story: An Argentine who emigrated to play in Spain and France, he learned several languages as a player and now coaches the United States in English.

name: Lionel Scaloni
story: He spent his playing career in Spain, Italy and England, absorbing languages before leading Argentina to glory.

name: Didier Deschamps
story: He played in Italy and England as a captain, picking up Italian and English before becoming France's most successful coach.

name: Roberto Martínez
story: A Spaniard who moved to England as a player, he is famed for his multilingual press conferences and now coaches Portugal.

name: Sarina Wiegman
story: A Dutch coach who once played college soccer in the United States, she leads England's Lionesses entirely in English.

name: Jorge Vilda
story: The Madrid-born coach guided Spain to the 2023 Women's World Cup title.

name: Pia Sundhage
story: A Swedish coaching legend, she led the USA and Sweden before learning Portuguese to manage Brazil.

name: Tony Gustavsson
story: A Swede who coached in the United States before leading co-hosts Australia to a historic World Cup semifinal at home.
